tasks.name: Save 5 short, high-rated science trivia facts to a 'Quick Science' favorites list
tasks.description: Find science trivia facts rated at least 4.5 stars and under 150 words (or marked as 'Short'), then add the first 5 that meet these criteria to a favorites list named 'Quick Science'. Success is having exactly 5 science trivia items in the 'Quick Science' favorites list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation menu, click on the 'Trivia' section.
On the Trivia page, use the category sidebar to click on 'Science'.
Locate the rating filter and set the minimum rating to 4.5 stars, then apply the filter.
Locate the length or reading time filter and select the option for short items (for example, 'Short (under 150 words)' or 'Under 1 minute').
From the filtered results, click on the first trivia fact card to open its detail page.
On the trivia detail page, click the 'Add to Favorites' button, choose 'Create New List', type 'Quick Science' in the list name field, and save the trivia to this new list.

Return to the filtered Science trivia results and, starting from the top, open the next four trivia fact cards one by one and click 'Add to Favorites', selecting the existing 'Quick Science' list each time until it contains 5 items.

tasks.name: Create a 'Lunch Break' playlist with jokes and quizzes totaling 12–18 minutes
tasks.description: Build a mixed session playlist named 'Lunch Break' by adding 3 jokes and enough quizzes so that the playlist’s total estimated duration is between 12 and 18 minutes. Success is having a playlist called 'Lunch Break' with exactly 3 jokes plus at least 2 quizzes and a displayed total time between 12–18 minutes.
steps: From any page, click the site logo to go to the homepage.
In the header navigation, click on 'Playlists' or 'My Sessions'.
On the playlists page, click the 'Create New Playlist' button.
In the playlist name field, type 'Lunch Break' and click the 'Save' or 'Create' button to create an empty playlist.
Use the navigation menu to go to the 'Jokes' section.
On the Jokes listing page, set the reading time filter to show jokes between 2 and 4 minutes and set the rating filter to show items with a minimum rating of 4.0 stars, then apply the filters.
From the filtered jokes list, click 'Add to Playlist' on the first three joke cards and select the 'Lunch Break' playlist each time.
Navigate to the 'Quizzes' section using the main navigation.

On the Quizzes listing page, set the completion time filter to 3–5 minutes and the difficulty filter to 'Easy' or 'Medium', then starting from the top of the filtered list, add quizzes one by one to the 'Lunch Break' playlist using the 'Add to Playlist' button until the playlist’s total duration indicator shows a value between 12 and 18 minutes.

tasks.name: Complete a 10-question Medium movie quiz and save the result as a note
tasks.description: Locate a movie-themed trivia quiz with Medium difficulty and exactly 10 questions, complete it by answering all questions, and save the result with a note titled 'Movie Quiz 10Q Medium'. Success is having one completed quiz result labeled or noted as 'Movie Quiz 10Q Medium' in your saved results or notes area.
steps: Go to the website homepage.
In the main navigation menu, click on the 'Quizzes' section.
On the Quizzes page, use the category filter to select 'Movies'.
Use the difficulty filter to select 'Medium' and apply it.
Use the question-count filter or options to select '10 questions' and apply it so that only 10-question quizzes are shown.
From the filtered list, click on the first quiz card that matches the 'Movies', 'Medium', and '10 questions' criteria to open the quiz.
For each of the 10 questions in the quiz, select the first answer option displayed and click the 'Next' or 'Submit' button until the quiz is finished and the results page is shown.
On the quiz results page, click the 'Add Note', 'Save Result', or similar button, type 'Movie Quiz 10Q Medium' into the title or note field, and save the result.

tasks.name: Follow the higher-rated category between 'Dad Jokes' and 'Puns' and save 3 top jokes
tasks.description: Compare the average category ratings of 'Dad Jokes' and 'Puns', follow the category with the higher average rating, and add 3 jokes rated at least 4.5 stars from that category to a favorites list. Success is following exactly one of these categories and having a favorites list named 'Best Dad Jokes' or 'Best Puns' containing 3 jokes from the chosen category.
steps: From the homepage, click on the 'Jokes' section in the main navigation.
In the jokes category menu or sidebar, click on 'Dad Jokes' to open that category page.
On the Dad Jokes category page, locate the displayed average rating for the category (for example, near the category name) and note its numerical value.
Use the category menu to switch to the 'Puns' category and locate the displayed average rating for this category as well.
Compare the two average rating numbers and decide which category, 'Dad Jokes' or 'Puns', has the higher rating.
Navigate to or stay on the category page that has the higher average rating.
On the chosen category page, click the 'Follow' or 'Subscribe' button located near the category title.
Within that category page, set the rating filter to show only jokes with a minimum rating of 4.5 stars and apply the filter.

From the filtered list, open the first three jokes one by one and click 'Add to Favorites' on each, creating a favorites list named 'Best Dad Jokes' or 'Best Puns' (matching the chosen category) for the first joke and adding the next two jokes to the same list.

tasks.name: Create a 'Saved for Work' collection with 3 recent random work jokes
tasks.description: Find jokes tagged 'Work' that were posted in the last 30 days, randomize their order, and save the first 3 random jokes to a collection named 'Saved for Work'. Success is having a collection titled 'Saved for Work' containing exactly 3 jokes tagged 'Work' from the last 30 days.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the 'Jokes' section in the main navigation menu.
On the Jokes page, use the tags filter to select the 'Work' tag and apply it.
Use the date or time filter to limit results to items posted in the 'Last 30 days' and apply the filter.
Click the 'Random', 'Shuffle', or equivalent button to randomize the display order of the filtered work jokes.
Open the first joke card in the randomized list and click the 'Save to Collection' or similar button, then create a new collection named 'Saved for Work' and save the joke to it.

Return to the randomized list of work jokes and add the next two jokes in order to the existing 'Saved for Work' collection by clicking the 'Save to Collection' button on each card and selecting 'Saved for Work'.

tasks.name: Customize homepage feed to 60% humor and 40% trivia with specific interests
tasks.description: Adjust feed preferences so that the homepage shows approximately 60% humorous content and 40% trivia, with interests set to Science, Movies, and Animals, and a preference for short content under 2 minutes. Success is saving these preference settings so they govern the homepage feed.
steps: From any page, click your profile icon or username in the site header.
In the dropdown menu, select 'Preferences' or 'Feed Settings' to open the personalization page.
On the preferences page, locate the humor/trivia balance slider and drag it until humor is set to 60% and trivia is set to 40%.
In the 'Topics' or 'Interests' section, deselect any currently selected topics, then select exactly three topics: 'Science', 'Movies', and 'Animals'.
In the 'Content Length' or 'Duration' section, select the option that indicates short content, such as 'Short (under 2 minutes)'.

In the 'Content Types' section, make sure both 'Jokes' and 'Trivia Facts' are checked, and leave any other optional content types (such as 'Long Articles' or 'Podcasts') unchecked if they are present.
Scroll to the bottom of the preferences page and click the 'Save Preferences' or 'Apply Settings' button.
Click the site logo or 'Home' link to return to the homepage, loading the main feed with the updated 60/40 humor-to-trivia balance and selected interests.

tasks.name: Comment on a popular space trivia fact and upvote two other comments
tasks.description: Find a space-related trivia fact with at least 100 likes, post a comment of at least 15 characters on it, and upvote two other users’ comments. Success is having one new comment from you on the selected trivia item and having given two upvotes to others’ comments.
steps: From the homepage, click on the 'Trivia' section in the main navigation.
In the categories sidebar on the Trivia page, select the 'Space' category.
Use the sort dropdown to choose 'Most Liked' so that the most liked space trivia items appear at the top.
From the sorted list, click on the first trivia fact whose like-count badge or indicator shows 100 or more likes to open its detail page.
Scroll down to the comments section of the trivia detail page and click inside the comment text box.
Type a comment that is at least 15 characters long, such as 'This space fact is really surprising!', and click the 'Post' or 'Submit' comment button.
In the comments list, scroll until you see comments posted by other users (not the one you just posted).
Click the 'Upvote', 'Like', or thumbs-up button on the first two other users’ comments you encounter in the list.

tasks.name: Save and rate the longest animal trivia about cats with reading time over 5 minutes
tasks.description: Search for animal trivia that mentions 'cat' in the title or content, filter for items with reading time of at least 5 minutes, then select the longest available item, save it to a 'Read Later' list, and rate it 5 stars. Success is having this single trivia item in the 'Read Later' list with a 5-star rating set by you.
steps: Go to the website homepage and click on the search bar in the header.
Type 'cat' into the search field and press Enter to run the search.
On the search results page, use the content type filter to select 'Trivia' and apply it.
Use the topic or tag filter to select 'Animals' and apply it to limit results to animal-related trivia.
Locate the reading time or length filter and set the minimum duration to '5 minutes' (or equivalent threshold), then apply the filter.
In the sort dropdown, select 'Length – Longest First' so that the longest trivia items appear at the top of the results.
Click on the first result in the sorted list to open the detailed trivia page about cats.

On the trivia detail page, click the 'Add to Read Later' or 'Save' button and choose or create a list named 'Read Later' if prompted, then click on the fifth star in the rating widget to set the rating to 5 stars.

entities.name: Joke
entities.description: Individual joke content items (e.g., Dad Jokes, Puns, Work jokes).

fields.name: categoryId
fields.description: ID of the JokeCategory (e.g., Dad Jokes, Puns).

fields.name: categoryName
fields.description: Denormalized display name of the joke category.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: List of free-form tags such as 'work'.

fields.name: ratingAverage
fields.description: Average star rating (e.g., 4.5).

fields.name: ratingCount
fields.description: Number of ratings that contributed to the average.

fields.name: userRating
fields.description: Current user’s rating for this joke (1–5), if set.

fields.name: readingTimeMinutes
fields.description: Estimated reading time in minutes (used for 2–4 minute filters).

fields.name: wordCount
fields.description: Approximate word count, useful for 'under 150 words' style filters.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: When the joke was posted (used for 'last 30 days' filters).

entities.name: JokeCategory
entities.description: Categories for jokes with aggregate metadata and follow state (e.g., Dad Jokes, Puns).

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name (e.g., 'Dad Jokes').

fields.name: slug
fields.description: Machine-readable identifier (e.g., 'dad_jokes', 'puns').

fields.name: averageRating
fields.description: Average rating across jokes in this category (used in Task 4).

fields.name: isFollowed
fields.description: Whether the current user follows/subscribes to this category.

entities.name: TriviaFact
entities.description: Individual trivia fact items (e.g., science facts, space facts, animal facts).

fields.name: category
fields.description: High-level topic such as 'science', 'space', 'animals'.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Topic tags (e.g., ['animals', 'cat']).

fields.name: ratingAverage
fields.description: Average star rating (used for ≥4.5 star filters).

fields.name: userRating
fields.description: Current user’s rating for this trivia fact (1–5).

fields.name: readingTimeMinutes
fields.description: Estimated reading time in minutes (used for 'short', '≥5 minutes' filters).

fields.name: wordCount
fields.description: Approximate word count, useful for 'under 150 words' logic.

fields.name: likesCount
fields.description: Total likes for the trivia fact (used to find items with ≥100 likes).

entities.name: Quiz
entities.description: Quiz definitions including topic, difficulty, and estimated completion time.

fields.name: topic
fields.description: Quiz category/topic such as 'movies', 'science'.

fields.name: difficulty
fields.description: Difficulty level used for filters (e.g., Medium).

fields.name: questionCount
fields.description: Number of questions (e.g., 10 for the movie quiz task).

fields.name: estimatedCompletionTimeMinutes
fields.description: Estimated quiz duration used in 3–5 minute filters and playlist calculations.

entities.name: QuizQuestion
entities.description: Individual questions belonging to a quiz.

fields.name: quizId
fields.description: ID of the parent Quiz.

fields.name: order
fields.description: Question order within the quiz (1..N).

fields.name: text
fields.description: Question prompt text.

fields.name: explanation
fields.description: Optional explanation shown on results.

entities.name: QuizAnswerOption
entities.description: Answer options for quiz questions, including which option is correct.

fields.name: questionId
fields.description: ID of the related QuizQuestion.

fields.name: order
fields.description: Display order of the answer option.

fields.name: isCorrect
fields.description: Whether this option is the correct answer.

entities.name: QuizResult
entities.description: Stored results of completed quizzes, including optional user note/title.

fields.name: quizId
fields.description: ID of the Quiz that was taken.

fields.name: quizTitle
fields.description: Denormalized quiz title for display in results library.

fields.name: scorePercent
fields.description: Score as a percentage (0–100).

fields.name: durationMinutes
fields.description: Actual time the user took to complete the quiz.

fields.name: noteTitle
fields.description: User-provided label or title (e.g., 'Movie Quiz 10Q Medium').

fields.name: noteBody
fields.description: Optional additional note text.

entities.name: Playlist
entities.description: User-defined playlists/sessions containing jokes, trivia, and quizzes (e.g., 'Lunch Break').

entities.name: PlaylistItem
entities.description: Items within a playlist, referencing jokes, trivia facts, or quizzes in a specific order.

fields.name: playlistId
fields.description: ID of the parent Playlist.

fields.name: contentType
fields.description: Type of content referenced by this playlist item.

fields.name: contentId
fields.description: ID of the referenced Joke, TriviaFact, or Quiz.

fields.name: order
fields.description: Position of the item in the playlist sequence (1..N).

entities.name: SavedList
entities.description: Named lists of saved items, covering favorites lists, collections, and read-later lists.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name (e.g., 'Quick Science', 'Best Dad Jokes', 'Saved for Work', 'Read Later').

fields.name: listType
fields.description: Functional type of the list.

entities.name: SavedListItem
entities.description: Membership of content items in a SavedList (favorites, collections, read later).

fields.name: savedListId
fields.description: ID of the parent SavedList.

fields.name: contentType
fields.description: Type of saved content.

fields.name: contentId
fields.description: ID of the referenced Joke, TriviaFact, or Quiz.

entities.name: FeedPreferences
entities.description: Configuration for the personalized homepage feed (humor/trivia balance, topics, formats).

fields.name: humorPercentage
fields.description: Percent of humor content in feed (e.g., 60).

fields.name: triviaPercentage
fields.description: Percent of trivia content in feed (e.g., 40).

fields.name: interests
fields.description: List of selected topic identifiers (e.g., ['science', 'movies', 'animals']).

fields.name: preferredContentLength
fields.description: Preferred content duration category.

fields.name: enabledContentTypes
fields.description: Array of allowed content type enums (e.g., ['jokes', 'trivia_facts']). Values come from feedContentTypeEnum.

entities.name: Comment
entities.description: Comments posted on content items, mainly trivia facts, with like/upvote counts.

fields.name: targetType
fields.description: Type of content the comment is attached to.

fields.name: targetId
fields.description: ID of the Joke, TriviaFact, or Quiz being commented on.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Comment body text (must be ≥15 characters for Task 7).

fields.name: authorName
fields.description: Display name of the comment author.

fields.name: isByCurrentUser
fields.description: True if this comment was posted by the current single user.

fields.name: likeCount
fields.description: Total upvotes/likes the comment has received.

fields.name: userHasUpvoted
fields.description: Whether the current user has upvoted this comment.
